title: Binary AI deployment failure mode
summary: Treating human-AI interaction as a two-body problem optimizes technical capability against user task while ignoring the latent coordination context, producing the 'brilliant tech, abandoned system' pattern.
source: Ch05 §Why Human-AI Interaction Requires the Third Body; Oxley on clinical BCI failure

nodes.label: User Task
nodes.description: The explicit job the tool is built for

edges.description: Better AI helps — up to a point
Context shapes what capability actually matters

identifiability_note: Coordination context (n4) confounds both the AI-capability lever and the deployment outcome. Without measuring it, the capability→adoption effect is biased and any A/B on pure capability overstates real-world value.
